svg.text: SOLID, as a well-run restaurant kitchen
The grill cook only cooks. They don't also plate desserts
or wash dishes — one role, one reason to change.
A new menu item is added as a new recipe card —
nobody has to rewrite how the grill station already works.
Any substitute grill cook can step in and the orders
still come out right — a substitute must honor the role.
Waiters get an order pad, not the whole kitchen manual —
nobody depends on parts of the system they never use.
The kitchen depends on "a produce supplier," not one
specific farm by name — swap the supplier, recipes don't change.
Same five ideas, applied to code instead of a kitchen.
svg.title: SOLID principles explained through five roles and habits in a well-run restaurant kitchen
